clause_text: Data Collection: We collect your name, email, phone number, and contacts from your phone. We also track your precise GPS location at all times to improve our service.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023.

The clause states that the company collects the user's name, email, phone number, and contacts from their phone, and tracks their precise GPS location at all times to improve their service. However, it does not specify that the consent given by the Data Principal is free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous, as required by Section 6(1) of the DPDP Act.

Specifically, the clause does not:

* Obtain explicit consent for the collection of contacts from the user's phone, which may not be necessary for the specified purpose of improving the service.
* Provide an option for the Data Principal to access the contents of the notice in a language specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution, as required by Section 3(3) of the DPDP Act.
* Limit the collection of personal data to what is necessary for the specified purpose, as required by Section 6(1) of the DPDP Act.

Suggested Correction:
The company should revise the clause to explicitly state that it obtains free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous consent from the user for the collection of their personal data, including contacts from their phone and GPS location. The company should also provide an option for the user to access the notice in a language of their choice and limit the collection of personal data to what is necessary for the specified purpose.

Revised Clause:
"We collect your name, email, and phone number with your explicit consent, which is free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous. We also request your consent to access your contacts from your phone, which is necessary for [ specify purpose, e.g., improving our service]. Additionally, we track your GPS location with your consent, which is necessary for [ specify purpose, e.g., providing location-based services]. You can access the contents of this notice in a language of your choice, as specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution."

clause_text: Third-Party Sharing: By using our app, you agree that we may share your personal data with our marketing partners, insurance companies, and any third-party advertisers we choose, without further notice to you.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023.

The exact Section of non-compliance is Section 6(1) and Section 5(1) of the DPDP Act, which states that consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous, and that every request for consent must be accompanied by a notice informing the Data Principal of the personal data and purpose of processing, among other things.

The Company Clause does not provide the required notice and does not obtain specific, informed, and unambiguous consent from the Data Principal. The clause also does not provide the Data Principal with the option to access the request in English or any language specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution.

Suggested Correction:
"By using our app, you agree that we may share your personal data with our marketing partners, insurance companies, and any third-party advertisers we choose. Before sharing your personal data, we will provide you with a notice in a clear and plain language, informing you of the personal data and purpose of sharing, and obtain your free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous consent. You will have the option to access this notice in English or any language specified in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution. You can withdraw your consent at any time, and we will provide you with the contact details of our Data Protection Officer to respond to any communication from you for the purpose of exercising your rights under the DPDP Act."

clause_text: Data Retention: We reserve the right to store your data indefinitely in our servers located in various international jurisdictions to ensure business continuity.
audit_result:
The Company Clause is Non-Compliant with the DPDP Act 2023. 

The exact Section of the DPDP Act that the Company Clause is non-compliant with is Section 8(7)(a), which states that a Data Fiduciary shall erase personal data upon the Data Principal withdrawing her consent or as soon as it is reasonable to assume that the specified purpose is no longer being served.

Suggested Correction: 
Data Retention: We will store your data for a limited period necessary to achieve the specified purpose. Upon completion of the specified purpose or withdrawal of your consent, we will erase your personal data unless retention is necessary for compliance with any law for the time being in force.

clause_text:
No Erasure: Due to the nature of our blockchain-based backend, we cannot delete or correct any data once it has been uploaded to our system.

Grievances: If you have any issues, you can email our general support team. We do not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer at this time.
audit_result:
1. Company Clause: No Erasure: Due to the nature of our blockchain-based backend, we cannot delete or correct any data once it has been uploaded to our system.
Compliance Status: Non-Compliant
Section: Section 12(3) of the DPDP Act 2023
Suggested Correction: Implement a mechanism to erase personal data upon request from the Data Principal, unless retention is necessary for compliance with any law or for the specified purpose.

2. Grievances: If you have any issues, you can email our general support team. We do not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer at this time.
Compliance Status: Non-Compliant
Section: Section 13(1) of the DPDP Act 2023
Suggested Correction: Establish a dedicated grievance redressal mechanism and appoint a Data Protection Officer to handle Data Principal grievances, and provide readily available means of grievance redressal. 

3. Legal Context: of any service or benefit sought by a Data Principal who is an employee.
Compliance Status: Partially Compliant
Section: Section 8(1) of the DPDP Act 2023
Suggested Correction: Ensure that the Data Fiduciary complies with the provisions of the DPDP Act 2023, including implementing technical and organizational measures to protect personal data, and responding to Data Principal requests for correction, completion, updating, and erasure of personal data.
